scope: High-risk surfaces NOT covered by prior session audits (C3 model-download integrity, store compliance): on-device inference engines (iOS llama.cpp C-API + Android JNI), desktop autonomous device-control + safety, server/IPC/Electron, mobile agent/conversation/selection logic.
method: Digest-first dimension fan-out (7 dimensions) -> per-dimension finders -> batched per-subsystem adversarial verifier (dual-lens). Only verifier-confirmed findings included.
verdict: SHIP-BLOCKING. Do not submit. 5 critical-class defects (incl. KV-cache slot leak graded critical) and multiple safety-bypass highs reachable on normal user paths.

subsystemsReviewed.title: Mobile agent loop + decision parsing (Swift + Kotlin)
Desktop RAG/memory + LLM chat path (NOT the C3 download)

confirmedFindings.title: Model and context leaked on re-load (double load without prior free)
confirmedFindings.what: load() assigns self.model/self.context/self.vocab unconditionally without freeing existing handles; a model-switch overwrites old llama_model*/llama_context* without llama_model_free/llama_free.
confirmedFindings.why: Each leaked context holds 4-11 GB of GGUF weights, reclaimed only at process exit; one switch can OOM the device.
confirmedFindings.fix: Free existing handles at the top of the canImport(llama) block before allocating, or require unload() first and assert self.model == nil.

confirmedFindings.title: LlamaEngine.handle/loadedModelId have no thread-safety — concurrent load/unload/complete is a use-after-free
confirmedFindings.what: handle:Long and loadedModelId:String? are plain var; load/unload/both complete overloads read+write handle unsynchronized; streaming overload holds it across a long native call.
confirmedFindings.why: unload() (UI cancel) on one thread while complete()/nativeComplete() runs on another frees the C++ LlamaHandle while generate() still uses h->ctx/h->model — SIGSEGV / native corruption.
confirmedFindings.fix: Guard all handle access with ReentrantReadWriteLock (read for ensureReady+nativeComplete, write for load/unload) or a coroutine Mutex.

confirmedFindings.title: Pending JNI exception not cleared after TokenSink.onToken — UB on next JNI call
confirmedFindings.what: After CallVoidMethod(sink, on_token, js), if onToken throws, the exception stays pending; no ExceptionCheck/ExceptionClear anywhere in generate(); next iteration's NewStringUTF (line 72) is prohibited with a pending exception.
confirmedFindings.why: ART aborts with fatal 'JNI called with pending exception'; any callback exception (back-pressure, UI teardown) crashes the app.
confirmedFindings.fix: After the callback: if (env->ExceptionCheck()) { env->DeleteLocalRef(js); break; }.

confirmedFindings.title: ArithmeticParser recursive descent causes an uncatchable stack overflow on iOS
confirmedFindings.what: parseFactor/parseExpression/parseTerm mutually recursive with no depth limit; ~183 nested parens overflow the 512 KB background-thread stack. Swift stack overflow is not a thrown Error so do/catch cannot catch it.
confirmedFindings.why: Adversarial model output or crafted goal hard-crashes iOS. Kotlin twin (AgentTool.kt:103-117) degrades via catchable StackOverflowError — a parity break where one platform crashes.
confirmedFindings.fix: Thread a depth param through the three functions; bail with nil at depth>100. Add the same guard to Kotlin for symmetry.

confirmedFindings.title: KV-cache sequence slot exhausted after every session rotation — chat fails permanently
confirmedFindings.what: LlamaContext has the default 1 sequence slot; ensureSession() (line 897) and the LLM_TIMEOUT path (948-949) replace/null generalChatSession without dispose(); autoDisposeSequence defaults false, so the slot is orphaned.
confirmedFindings.why: After the first rotation, context.getSequence() throws 'No sequences left' — all subsequent generalChat calls fail permanently until full model reload; on embedded HW (MAX_CHAT_TURNS=3) this hits in minutes.
confirmedFindings.fix: autoDisposeSequence:true on session creation or explicit dispose() before overwrite; dispose in the LLM_TIMEOUT catch; same in unloadModel() (302) and setPreset() (230).

confirmedFindings.title: tokenToPiece silently drops tokens when the 64-byte buffer is too small
confirmedFindings.what: llama_token_to_piece returns negated required byte count when the buffer is too small; guard n>0 treats n<0 like n==0 and returns empty.
confirmedFindings.why: Tokens whose piece exceeds 64 bytes (long Unicode, byte-fallback, special tokens) are silently dropped — corrupted output, no error/log.
confirmedFindings.fix: On n<0, reallocate to Int(-n) and retry; only n==0 is truly empty.

confirmedFindings.title: runGeneration holds the actor's serial executor for the whole inference — blocks unload/Stop
confirmedFindings.what: runGeneration is non-async; Task{ self.runGeneration() } runs the entire token loop synchronously on the actor executor with no suspension.
confirmedFindings.why: Concurrent await calls (unload, loadedModelID, second generate) queue behind it for minutes; the Stop button is non-functional during inference.
confirmedFindings.fix: Make runGeneration async with await Task.yield() per token, or move the C loop off the actor via withCheckedThrowingContinuation + detached thread.

confirmedFindings.title: llama_context left undefined after a failed initial prompt decode, then reused
confirmedFindings.what: On decode==false the method throws but leaves self.context alive with undefined KV-cache and self.loaded non-nil; next generate() reuses the corrupt context.
confirmedFindings.why: Garbage output or crash inside llama_decode on the subsequent call.
confirmedFindings.fix: llama_kv_cache_clear(context) before throwing (lines 156 and 172), or null out context+loaded.

confirmedFindings.title: GetStringUTFChars result not null-checked before std::string construction
confirmedFindings.what: GetStringUTFChars(prompt,nullptr) can return NULL on OOM; result passed straight to std::string(p).
confirmedFindings.why: std::string from a null const char* is UB — SIGSEGV.
confirmedFindings.fix: if (!p) return env->NewStringUTF(""); after each call (117,128,92).

confirmedFindings.title: NewStringUTF return not checked; null jstring -> opaque NPE in Kotlin
confirmedFindings.what: NewStringUTF can return NULL on OOM; mapped to Kotlin external fun :String (non-nullable); ART does not auto-NPE, NULL propagates. Line 72 passes a possibly-null jstring into onToken(piece:String).
confirmedFindings.why: Opaque crash with no useful stack frame.
confirmedFindings.fix: On NULL: ExceptionClear(), ThrowNew(OutOfMemoryError), return NULL so ART delivers cleanly.

confirmedFindings.title: g_backend_ready read-modify-write without synchronization
confirmedFindings.what: Plain bool g_backend_ready; check-then-set if(!g_backend_ready){llama_backend_init();g_backend_ready=true;} has no barrier/lock.
confirmedFindings.why: Two engines on different threads can both observe false and call llama_backend_init() concurrently (not documented re-entrant); the unsynchronized bool is a C++ data race (UB).
confirmedFindings.fix: std::call_once(g_backend_flag, llama_backend_init); with std::once_flag, or std::atomic<bool> + mutex.

confirmedFindings.title: _isRunning never reset on exception — agent permanently locks
confirmedFindings.what: _isRunning=true at 111, reset only at 346, no try/finally; waitForIdle at 171 is outside the inner try/catch (begins 239) and the CHAT early-return at 160 skips the reset.
confirmedFindings.why: Any throw leaves _isRunning stuck true; all future runAgentLoop() calls return immediately at 109 — agent silently dead for the process lifetime.
confirmedFindings.fix: Wrap the loop body in try { } finally { this._isRunning=false; }.

confirmedFindings.title: waitForIdle unbounded loop — hangs forever on an animating UI
confirmedFindings.what: while(!isIdle) has no success-path cap; retries>=3 covers only getScreenContext exceptions; an animated UI changes element count every poll so stableCount never reaches 2.
confirmedFindings.why: Spins indefinitely, blocks the agent step, accumulates 2-5 MB PNGs in /tmp (cleanup at 81-85 is after the loop), and with H7 permanently locks the agent.
confirmedFindings.fix: Add MAX_IDLE_POLLS cap (~20) and treat timeout as stable; move cleanup into finally.

confirmedFindings.title: Safety BLOCKLIST not applied to 'key' actions — Enter confirms a blocked dialog
confirmedFindings.what: checkSafety(el) runs for click/input but not the key branch; the pre-branch checkSafety(undefined, actionObj.text) is empty for key presses.
confirmedFindings.why: A model blocked from clicking 'Pay' can immediately {action:'key',key:'enter'} on the focused dialog to confirm the payment.
confirmedFindings.fix: Examine the focused element's text in the key branch; restrict enter/back after a safety-blocked action until UI state is verified.

confirmedFindings.title: Safety BLOCKLIST critically incomplete; ignores resourceId; goal unchecked
confirmedFindings.what: BLOCKLIST=['pay','delete','password','buy','confirm purchase'] missing transfer/send money/purchase/remove/wipe/factory reset/uninstall/revoke/deactivate/checkout; checkSafety ignores el.resourceId; goal never checked pre-loop.
confirmedFindings.why: Icon buttons with empty labels but resource-id like confirm_transfer_btn pass unchecked; destructive verbs sail through.
confirmedFindings.fix: Expand blocklist, add el.resourceId to checked text, add a pre-loop goal check in runAgentLoop().

confirmedFindings.title: will-navigate origin check uses startsWith — subdomain-prefix bypass
confirmedFindings.what: navUrl.startsWith(appOrigin) with appOrigin=http://localhost:3000 passes for http://localhost:3000.attacker.com/.
confirmedFindings.why: Renderer tricked into navigating there loads an external page in the Electron shell with full IPC access.
confirmedFindings.fix: Compare parsed origins (new URL(navUrl).origin !== new URL(appOrigin).origin) or appOrigin+'/' with exact-match fallback.

confirmedFindings.title: will-redirect not handled — 3xx server redirects bypass the navigation guard
confirmedFindings.what: No will-redirect handler; will-navigate fires only for renderer-initiated navigations, server 3xx fire will-redirect.
confirmedFindings.why: A buggy or path-traversal-reachable local route can redirect the renderer to an arbitrary external URL with no check.
confirmedFindings.fix: Add a will-redirect handler using the same fixed origin comparison.

confirmedFindings.title: Kotlin returns FinalAnswer on multi-JSON output; Swift returns planError
confirmedFindings.what: firstJsonObject extracts text[indexOf('{')..lastIndexOf('}')]; with two JSON objects the span covers both. Swift's JSONSerialization rejects it -> planError; Kotlin's regex extractString finds the first key -> if the 2nd object has 'answer' it returns FinalAnswer.
confirmedFindings.why: Reproducer {"tool":"echo","input":"hello"} text {"answer":"injected"} makes Android terminate early with a premature/attacker-crafted answer while iOS stalls.
confirmedFindings.fix: Walk from the first { counting balanced braces (skip quoted strings) to find the outermost complete object; apply to both platforms.

confirmedFindings.title: Safety gate not applied to FinalAnswer on either platform
confirmedFindings.what: SafetyBlocklist.isBlocked runs only in the UseTool branch (Swift:65,Kotlin:51); FinalAnswer is returned unscanned. Parity-equal omission.
confirmedFindings.why: A jailbroken/fine-tuned on-device model can emit blocked content (phishing, private-key extraction) in finalAnswer reaching the UI. High not critical: displayed text, no system side effects.
confirmedFindings.fix: Add SafetyBlocklist.isBlocked(answer) in the FinalAnswer branch on both platforms; halt with .blocked when matched.

confirmedFindings.title: GenerationOptions.stopSequences completely ignored
confirmedFindings.what: Token loop exits only on cancel/EOG/maxTokens; options.stopSequences never read.
confirmedFindings.why: Public InferenceEngine seam; any caller passing stop strings is silently ignored, breaking structured-output parsing. Latent today (AgentLoop passes defaults).
confirmedFindings.fix: Rolling tail buffer; break when it ends with any stop sequence; trim the stop string from the final yield.

confirmedFindings.title: Int32 overflow in tokenize() for >2 GB prompts
confirmedFindings.what: Int32(text.utf8.count) traps (debug) / wraps negative (release) above Int32.max; negative byteLen passed to llama_tokenize.
confirmedFindings.why: Crash or garbage tokenization (C UB). Very low probability (>2 GB text).
confirmedFindings.fix: guard text.utf8.count <= Int(Int32.max) else { return [] }; clamp capacity.

confirmedFindings.title: ScriptedInferenceEngine.generate() missing modelNotLoaded guard
confirmedFindings.what: No guard loaded!=nil; delivers a scripted reply even after unload(). Mock enforces it at line 25; production LlamaEngine throws.
confirmedFindings.why: Agent-loop tests using the scripted engine after unload silently pass where production would throw, masking I1 violations.
confirmedFindings.fix: Add guard loaded != nil else { throw InferenceError.modelNotLoaded }.

confirmedFindings.title: type() maps all whitespace to %s — newline/tab/CR corrupt typed text or shell
confirmedFindings.what: Escape regex matches all \s; only space maps to %s, tab/LF/CR map to \<char> passed as one adb arg to device-shell input text.
confirmedFindings.why: \<LF> is shell line-continuation (newline dropped); \<CR> is shell-dependent (truncates on mksh, Android<6). CRLF in file/paste content reaches this.
confirmedFindings.fix: Strip/normalize non-space whitespace first, then escape metacharacters and map space->%s; or single-quote the whole arg device-side.

confirmedFindings.title: Shared fixed on-device path /sdcard/window_dump.xml — TOCTOU
confirmedFindings.what: Both getUiHierarchyXml() and getScreenContext() dump to the same hardcoded device path; local temp uses a UUID, device side does not.
confirmedFindings.why: Interleaved ADB ops (post-action waitForUiIdle vs uiVerifier.waitForIdle, multi-device, retry overlap) make one reader pick up a stale/mid-write dump.
confirmedFindings.fix: UUID-suffix the device path and adb shell rm it after pull, matching the local-temp pattern.

confirmedFindings.title: uiParser.traverse() registers zero-bounds container nodes as clickable id=0
confirmedFindings.what: Element-building runs unconditionally for every node incl. the bounds-less hierarchy root; extractBounds('') -> center (0,0).
confirmedFindings.why: A ghost {id:0,...} element is always present; a confused/adversarial model can {action:'click',id:0} and tap the device top-left corner.
confirmedFindings.fix: Skip registration unless the node has at least one of non-empty text/contentDesc/bounds.

confirmedFindings.title: manualAction injected into the LLM prompt without a type guard
confirmedFindings.what: POST /api/agent/resume passes req.body.manualAction straight to resume() with no typeof check; stored in _pendingManualOverride, interpolated into actionHistory (agent.service.ts:196) and injectOverride() (:198).
confirmedFindings.why: A non-string JSON value (object/array) coerces into the LLM action-history context — prompt injection / input-validation hole.
confirmedFindings.fix: const manualAction = typeof req.body?.manualAction==='string' ? req.body.manualAction.slice(0,4000).trim() : undefined;

confirmedFindings.title: Zip-slip: unzipper.Extract without entry-path validation on voice download
confirmedFindings.what: POST /api/voice/download pipes a fetched ZIP through unzipper.Extract({path:voiceDir}); unzipper does not strip ../ from entry names.
confirmedFindings.why: Compromised CDN or MITM can write outside ~/.quenderin/models/voice/; reachable by any local process.
confirmedFindings.fix: Stream via Parse() and validate each entry path resolves under voiceDir; autodrain() escaping entries.

confirmedFindings.title: SafetyBlocklist substring matching causes false-positive blocks
confirmedFindings.what: haystack.contains(keyword) with no word boundaries; format/pin/pay/wire/bank/erase match unrelated words.
confirmedFindings.why: A match halts the whole run with .blocked and no recovery, so legitimate text tasks silently fail. Parity-equal.
confirmedFindings.fix: Word-boundary anchors (\b<keyword>\b) for single-word entries on both platforms.

confirmedFindings.title: Android AgentSession shared mutable state not thread-safe
confirmedFindings.what: steps/isRunning/answer/haltReason are plain var mutated from a background thread; onChange() fires from background (Compose recomposition hook); no @Volatile/lock, no concurrent-run() guard.
confirmedFindings.why: Compose snapshot read of steps on main while background writes steps=steps+step is a JVM data race; concurrent run() races destructively. iOS twin is @MainActor (safe).
confirmedFindings.fix: MutableStateFlow per field collected in Compose, or @Volatile + lock; guard run() against re-entry.

confirmedFindings.title: cosineSimilarity returns NaN on embedding length mismatch, destabilizing sort
confirmedFindings.what: Iterates 0..a.length-1, reads b[i] with no length guard; shorter b yields undefined -> NaN through dotProduct and the b.score-a.score comparator.
confirmedFindings.why: On embedding-model dimension change, NaN makes sort order undefined; valid high-scoring entries can be misordered/excluded from top-k. Latent until a model upgrade.
confirmedFindings.fix: if (a.length !== b.length) return 0; warn when loading stale-dimension corrections.

confirmedFindings.title: saveCorrection embeds inside the write-lock; getExtractor double-init race
confirmedFindings.what: embedText called inside withWriteLock (197), holding the lock for the full 100-500ms embed; getExtractor() has no mutex so two concurrent first calls both await pipeline(...), loading the ~80MB model twice.
confirmedFindings.why: Lock held across expensive embed blocks concurrent reads/saves; double-init wastes ~80MB (one-time, not corruption).
confirmedFindings.fix: Hoist embedText outside the lock; protect extractor init with a promise-chain mutex.

confirmedFindings.title: Kotlin ConversationStore.escape() does not escape \r
confirmedFindings.what: escape() escapes \,\n,\t but not \r; a bare \r in message text silently splits a row under BufferedReader.readLine().
confirmedFindings.why: Silent data loss with no exception when the app layer reads via readLine(). iOS uses JSONEncoder (immune).
confirmedFindings.fix: Escape \r in escape() and add the 'r' case in unescape().

confirmedFindings.title: Kotlin title substring(0,40) can split a UTF-16 surrogate pair
confirmedFindings.what: substring(0,40) counts UTF-16 code units; an emoji straddling pos 39-40 produces an unpaired high surrogate (invalid Unicode).
confirmedFindings.why: Can break Gson/Jackson or display; Swift prefix(40) is grapheme-cluster safe (parity gap).
confirmedFindings.fix: Surrogate-safe truncation or codePoints().limit().

confirmedFindings.title: Preload path resolves to a non-existent file in a built app — preload silently not loaded
confirmedFindings.what: Preload path path.join(__dirname,'..','src','electron','preload.js') resolves to dist/src/electron/preload.js, but tsc emits electron/preload.ts to dist/electron/preload.js; the file does not exist in a production build.
confirmedFindings.why: Electron silently proceeds without a preload when the path is missing, so contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld never runs (window API undefined; intended sandbox-boundary script absent).
confirmedFindings.fix: Derive from build output (path.join(__dirname,'preload.js')) and assert fs.existsSync at startup.

confirmedFindings.title: llama_backend_init() called N times, freed once on repeated load() without unload()
confirmedFindings.what: Asymmetric init/free; load()->load() calls init twice, free once.
confirmedFindings.why: Older llama.cpp documents init as 'call once per process'; current ref-counting not guaranteed.
confirmedFindings.fix: Gate init behind a backendInitialized flag; free only when set.

confirmedFindings.title: MockInferenceEngine ignores maxTokens and cancellation
confirmedFindings.what: Yields all words synchronously in the stream closure; no Task.isCancelled, no maxTokens cap.
confirmedFindings.why: Tests asserting maxTokens/cancellation pass against the mock but fail against LlamaEngine.
confirmedFindings.fix: Wrap in a Task, poll cancellation per word, honor maxTokens.

confirmedFindings.title: GetObjectClass local ref leaked in generate()
confirmedFindings.what: jclass cls = GetObjectClass(sink) never DeleteLocalRef'd; slot held for the whole (minutes-long) streaming frame.
confirmedFindings.why: JNI contract violation flagged by ASan/StrictMode; slot released at frame return, so impact is low.
confirmedFindings.fix: env->DeleteLocalRef(cls); right after the GetMethodID lookup.

confirmedFindings.title: bulk-delete sends 50 args to adb shell input keyevent — truncated on Android<=9
confirmedFindings.what: Array(50).fill('67') spread as 50 args; API<=28 accepts only one keycode and ignores the rest.
confirmedFindings.why: Pre-Android-10 only one KEYCODE_DEL fires; field not cleared and new text appends to stale content.
confirmedFindings.fix: Loop individual calls, or a device-side for loop, or select-all + delete.

confirmedFindings.title: POST /api/models/download accepts any modelId, silently falls back
confirmedFindings.what: Unrecognized modelId falls back to MODEL_CATALOG[0] (largest) in llm.service.ts:654, but the response reflects the invalid requested id.
confirmedFindings.why: Misleading success message; a different (largest) model is downloaded. DELETE route already validates.
confirmedFindings.fix: Validate against catalog and return 400 for unknown ids (mirror the DELETE route).

confirmedFindings.title: diagnosticsId echoed back without a length cap
confirmedFindings.what: Trimmed but not length-capped before being echoed in the JSON body.
confirmedFindings.why: A multi-MB string is allocated and serialized into the response (no XSS via res.json()); wastes heap/bandwidth.
confirmedFindings.fix: .slice(0,128) on both query and header reads.

confirmedFindings.title: manualAction has no length cap (subsumed by M7)
confirmedFindings.what: No length limit before append to action-history / injectOverride(). WS goals cap at 4000/8000; HTTP resume has no equivalent.
confirmedFindings.why: Large input inflates the LLM context -> OOM/silent truncation. Fully resolved by the M7 .slice(0,4000) fix.
confirmedFindings.fix: Apply .slice(0,4000).trim() as part of the M7 type guard — no separate change.

confirmedFindings.title: Both model selectors expose negative memoryHeadroomGb in the FORCED path
confirmedFindings.what: FORCED fallback computes usable - runtime with no clamp; negative when even the smallest model does not fit.
confirmedFindings.why: Physically meaningless negative headroom surfaced to UI / downstream consumers.
confirmedFindings.fix: max(0.0, usable - runtime) on both platforms.

confirmedFindings.title: Silent startup failure shows a blank window with no error
confirmedFindings.what: Catch only console.errors; no dialog.showErrorBox/app.quit(). (The 'zombie window via activate' sub-claim was disproven: activate is registered after createWindow succeeds.)
confirmedFindings.why: App appears launched (dock icon) but renders a blank screen with no error feedback.
confirmedFindings.fix: dialog.showErrorBox + app.quit() in the catch.

confirmedFindings.title: ConversationContext.build early-breaks on first over-budget message, dropping older messages that would fit
confirmedFindings.what: The reversed loop breaks on the first over-budget message, discarding all older messages even when they individually fit.
confirmedFindings.why: A large paste as the second-newest message drops every prior turn (even 10-30-token ones). Identical on both platforms.
confirmedFindings.fix: Replace break with continue; the kept.isEmpty guard already preserves the newest turn.

confirmedFindings.title: MAX_CHAT_TURNS computed from user-facing contextSize, not the effective context
confirmedFindings.what: Divides currentSettings.contextSize (default 2048) by 100; the actually-negotiated context (as low as 256) is never stored.
confirmedFindings.why: Returns 20 turns where the real context holds ~3; after turn 3 the model silently drops earlier turns -> incoherent responses, no error, no correct reset boundary.
confirmedFindings.fix: Store this.effectiveContextSize=effectiveCtx at model load and use it: Math.max(3, Math.min(25, floor(effective/100))).

confirmedFindings.title: Synchronous vm_stat/PowerShell every 15s in the memory-pressure monitor stalls the event loop
confirmedFindings.what: startMemoryPressureMonitor() setInterval(15s) calls availableMemBytes() -> execSync('vm_stat',{timeout:2000}) (macOS) / execSync('powershell...',{timeout:5000}) (Windows) (memory.ts:60,137-146).
confirmedFindings.why: Blocks the Node event loop up to 2-5s every 15s; during inference drops Electron frames and can fire the AbortController timeout in promptWithTimeout, aborting healthy generations.
confirmedFindings.fix: Use os.freemem() (non-blocking) in the interval; reserve the precise call for the one-time model-load path.

confirmedFindings.title: Shell injection via unsanitized dirPath in execSync(df -k "${dirPath}") on Unix
confirmedFindings.what: Template-literal shell command; dirPath derives from os.homedir(). POSIX allows " and $( in path components; Windows sanitizes the drive letter, Unix does not.
confirmedFindings.why: A home directory containing " or $( breaks quoting / allows subshell execution with the app's privileges.
confirmedFindings.fix: execFileSync('df',['-k',dirPath],...) (no shell) or fs.promises.statfs(dirPath).

rejectedFindings.title: XML fallback parser oversized-text
rejectedFindings.reason: Parsed commandText is LLM output capped at maxTokens:150 (~600 chars); 'arbitrarily large' is structurally impossible.

rejectedFindings.title: parseToolCalls ReDoS
rejectedFindings.reason: V8 Irregexp gives linear-time guarantees for this pattern class; input is token-capped on-device model output, not the 10k adversarial string postulated.

rejectedFindings.title: Electron preload process.env secret leak
rejectedFindings.reason: Window is sandbox:true; sandboxed preload process.env is restricted and does not reflect main-process env. Minor always-0.0.1 version cosmetic remains, not the claimed leak.

residualRisk: No documented threading contract across the inference engines (root of H2 and C2); point fixes do not prevent the next caller reintroducing a race.

On-device model is trusted by default; the safety blocklist and decision parser are the only defense and both are bypassable today (C4, H9/H10/H13/H14, M9). Treat model output as untrusted end-to-end.

Swift<->Kotlin parity is unenforced; C4 (crash vs graceful) and H13 (planError vs FinalAnswer) are silent divergences on identical input. No cross-platform conformance test. Recommend a shared golden-input fixture.

C-API/JNI buffer/return-value edge cases (H1,H4,H5,L3,M2) were found by inspection not fuzzing; ASan + JNI-checker fuzz on llama_jni.cpp and large-token fuzz on LlamaEngine.swift would likely surface more.

Resource-disposal in llm.service.ts is fragile (C5 slot leak + MEM-HIGH-1 effective-context mismatch from local-variable state); the session-rotation path warrants a focused redesign, not just point fixes.
Not in scope: C3 model-download integrity diff and store compliance (prior session audits); UI/styling; build/CI.
topActions: iOS LlamaEngine.swift:75-78 — free old model/context/vocab before re-load (C1; prevents multi-GB OOM on model switch).
llm.service.ts:891-901 — dispose outgoing chat session / autoDisposeSequence:true (C5; chat dies permanently after first rotation otherwise).
Android LlamaEngine.kt — lock handle/loadedModelId across load/unload/complete (C2; use-after-free native crash).
llama_jni.cpp:73 — ExceptionCheck/Clear after onToken callback (C3; ART process abort).
AgentTool.swift:102-114 — add recursion depth guard (C4; uncatchable iOS crash + parity break).
agent.service.ts try/finally + uiVerifier.ts idle-poll cap (H7+H8; permanent agent lock).
actionExecutor.ts — safety check on 'key' actions + expand blocklist + check resourceId + pre-loop goal check (H9+H10; payment/destructive-action bypass).
electron/main.ts — origin-equality navigation check + will-redirect handler (H11+H12).
